---
title: "Builder profile"
description: "Full AdministratorAccess on the agent instance — the heavy-hitter profile."
---

<Warning>
The builder profile attaches **AWS managed `AdministratorAccess`** to the EC2 instance. Any code the agent runs has full access to the account. **Only deploy builder into a dedicated sandbox account** — never your production account.
</Warning>

## What it is

The builder profile is for agents that need to *create* AWS resources: VPCs, Lambda, CloudFront, CodeBuild, EKS, whatever. It's the right choice if you want the agent to deploy your code end-to-end.

## Defaults (simple mode)

| Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| IAM policy | `arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AdministratorAccess` |
| Instance type | `t4g.xlarge` (4 vCPU, 16 GiB, arm64) |
| Root volume | 40 GB |
| Data volume | 80 GB (OpenClaw) / 0 GB (stateless packs) |
| Security Hub | ✅ enabled |
| GuardDuty | ✅ enabled |
| Inspector | ✅ enabled |
| Access Analyzer | ✅ enabled |
| Config recorder | ✅ enabled |
| SSH access | disabled (SSM only) |

## Install examples

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="OpenClaw + builder">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack openclaw \
      --profile builder
    ```
    Gives you a persistent OpenClaw gateway that can deploy anything.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Claude Code + builder">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack claude-code \
      --profile builder
    ```
    Claude Code with full AWS admin via Bedrock.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Codex + builder">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack codex-cli \
      --profile builder
    ```
    OpenAI Codex with AWS admin. Runs as builder agent (`danger-full-access` sandbox, never prompts). SSM in and run `codex login` post-deploy.
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Guardrails that come with builder

All five AWS security services auto-enable. They run in **alert-only** mode — they won't block the agent, but will tell you if it does something suspicious:

- **Security Hub** aggregates findings from GuardDuty, Inspector, Access Analyzer
- **GuardDuty** flags anomalous API calls (crypto-mining, credential exfiltration patterns)
- **Inspector** continuously scans EC2 + Lambda + ECR for CVEs
- **Access Analyzer** flags cross-account exposure (public S3, over-permissioned roles)
- **Config recorder** captures every resource change for audit

Plus the instance has no inbound SSH (`SSHAllowedCidr=127.0.0.1/32`). You reach it via [SSM Session Manager](/reference/cli#connect-via-ssm).

## When NOT to use builder

- **Production accounts** — too much blast radius.
- **Shared accounts** — the agent has keys to everything your colleagues do.
- **NemoClaw** — incompatible; NemoClaw requires `personal_assistant`.

Use [account_assistant](/profiles/account-assistant) for a read-heavy agent or [personal_assistant](/profiles/personal-assistant) for a no-AWS agent.
